schools.summary: Gill: the king and inhabitants shut it up within, with iron doors and bars of brass, blocked up without by Israel; none went out or in.

themes: Now Jericho was straitly shut up... none went out, and none came in
the seeming impossibility setting off God's power
not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit (Zech. 4:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the same divine Person called the Captain of the Lord's host now gives Joshua these orders and assurances.
Keil & Delitzsch: I have given Jericho into thine hand.

themes: See, I have given into thine hand Jericho, and the king thereof
the victory granted by God's word before the battle
God giveth us the victory through Christ (1 Cor. 15:57)

schools.summary: Gill: all able for war were to compass the city, not in a siege but by a procession around it.
Keil & Delitzsch: compass the city once, six days.

themes: ye shall compass the city... once. Thus shalt thou do six days
the strange means a test of obedience and faith
the foolishness of God is wiser than men (1 Cor. 1:25)

schools.summary: Gill, with Ben Gersom: the ark, carried by priests, directed Israel to keep all written in the law contained in it.
Keil & Delitzsch: the seventh day they were to compass seven times.

themes: seven priests shall bear before the ark seven trumpets of rams' horns... the seventh day ye shall compass the city seven times
God's presence and word at the centre of the conquest
let God arise, let his enemies be scattered (Ps. 68:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the long blast, drawn out, was made only on the seventh day, different from the short blasts; the wall should fall down flat and the people go up.

themes: all the people shall shout with a great shout; and the wall of the city shall fall down flat
the wall falling before faith, not force
by faith the walls of Jericho fell down (Heb. 11:30)

schools.summary: Gill: not the Levites and Kohathites whose work it commonly was, but the priests on this occasion, as many as sufficient.
Keil & Delitzsch: the communicating of the command.

themes: Take up the ark of the covenant, and let seven priests bear seven trumpets of rams' horns
prompt and exact obedience to the command
done according to all the LORD commanded (Exod. 39:42)

schools.summary: Gill: the people here are the women, children, and unarmed, distinguished from the armed men.
Keil & Delitzsch: the people passing on to compass the city.

themes: Pass on, and compass the city, and let him that is armed pass on before the ark of the LORD
the whole congregation in the procession of faith
compassed about seven days (Heb. 11:30)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua had finished the orders and directions given the people; the priests blew and the ark of the covenant followed.

themes: the seven priests... passed on before the LORD, and blew with the trumpets: and the ark of the covenant... followed
the procession a sacred act before the LORD
the LORD with the sound of a trumpet (Ps. 47:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the armed men, some interpret of Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh, others of all the armed men; the rereward after the ark, the trumpets blowing continually.

themes: the armed men went before the priests... and the rereward came after the ark
God's presence at the heart of the host
the angel of the LORD encampeth round about them (Ps. 34:7)

schools.summary: Gill: this was given when he ordered them to compass the city.
Wesley: shouting before the time would be ineffectual, discouraging themselves and giving the enemy matter of insult.

themes: Ye shall not shout... until the day I bid you shout; then shall ye shout
the discipline of patient, exact obedience
he that believeth shall not make haste (Isa. 28:16)

schools.summary: Gill: the ark, borne by the priests, was carried round the city, or, as Kimchi, Joshua caused the ark to compass it; they came into the camp and lodged.

themes: So the ark of the LORD compassed the city, going about it once... and lodged in the camp
faith continuing though no result yet appears
need of patience to receive the promise (Heb. 10:36)

schools.summary: Gill: he rose early to direct and prepare everything for another procession; so active and diligent was he to do the will and work of God.
Keil & Delitzsch: Joshua rose early in the morning.

themes: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests took up the ark of the LORD
the leader's diligence and zeal in God's work
earnest, punctual obedience to the command

schools.summary: Gill: refers to the order already given.
Keil & Delitzsch: the priests going on continually and blowing the trumpets, the armed men before.

themes: seven priests... before the ark of the LORD went on continually, and blew with the trumpets
the patient constancy of faith, day after day
let us not be weary in well doing (Gal. 6:9)

schools.summary: Gill: they went round once only, as on the first.
Clarke: doubts the whole host went each day, the fighting men alone being a vast number.

themes: the second day they compassed the city once... so they did six days
the steady walk of faith through delay
though it tarry, wait for it (Hab. 2:3)

schools.summary: Gill: some Jews say it was the sabbath; whether or not, one of the seven days must have been a sabbath; they compassed it seven times that day.

themes: on the seventh day... they rose early... and compassed the city after the same manner seven times
the climax of patient obedience
reaping in due season if we faint not (Gal. 6:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the seventh time of going round on the seventh day.
Henry: the people had religiously observed the orders, and now Joshua told them the LORD had given them the city.

themes: Joshua said unto the people, Shout; for the LORD hath given you the city
the shout of faith claiming the victory on God's word
always caused to triumph (2 Cor. 2:14)

schools.summary: Gill: 'accursed' is a 'cherem,' devoted to the Lord.
Henry: the people had observed the orders, and now were told the LORD had given the city.

themes: the city shall be accursed... to the LORD: only Rahab the harlot shall live... because she hid the messengers
judgment exact yet mercy rescuing the believing
the harlot perished not with the unbelieving (Heb. 11:31)

schools.summary: Gill: they were to keep from laying hold on, secreting, or enjoying as their own what was devoted to another use; taking it would make themselves and the camp accursed.

themes: keep yourselves from the accursed thing... and make the camp of Israel a curse, and trouble it
what is devoted to God never turned to selfish use
one man's hidden sin troubling all the camp (Josh. 7:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the silver, gold, and vessels of brass and iron.
Clarke: they were to come into the treasury of the LORD.

themes: all the silver, and gold, and vessels of brass and iron, are consecrated unto the LORD
the firstfruits of conquest wholly God's
honour the LORD with the firstfruits (Prov. 3:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the people shouted as Joshua had charged them.
Clarke: much labour spent to prove the shout might naturally cause the fall, but the text ascribes it to God.

themes: the people shouted... that the wall fell down flat... and they took the city
the stronghold falling by God's power at the shout of faith
by faith the walls of Jericho fell down (Heb. 11:30)

schools.summary: Gill: all the inhabitants destroyed by Joshua's direction and God's order, being guilty of capital crimes — idolatry, incest, and the like.

themes: they utterly destroyed all that was in the city... with the edge of the sword
the cherem of God's righteous judgment
the iniquity of the Amorite now full (Gen. 15:16)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua had said this to the spies before the people entered, perhaps before the walls fell; to bring her out as they sware unto her.

themes: Go into the harlot's house, and bring out thence the woman... as ye sware unto her
the oath faithfully kept amid the destruction
the LORD is faithful, who shall stablish you (2 Thess. 3:3)

schools.summary: Gill: the spies knew her house again by the scarlet thread hung at the window; they left them without the camp of Israel.

themes: brought out Rahab, and her father, and her mother, and her brethren, and all that she had
faith saving a household, marked by the scarlet thread
when I see the blood, I will pass over you (Exod. 12:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the city burnt with fire, as Babylon the great, of which it was an emblem, will be burnt.
Clarke: only the silver and gold they put into the treasury.

themes: they burnt the city with fire... only the silver, and the gold... they put into the treasury of the house of the LORD
the doomed city given to the fire, what is God's preserved
Babylon utterly burned with fire (Rev. 18:8)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua saved her from the sword; Josephus says he gave her fields and held her in great honour; she dwelt in Israel for hiding the messengers.

themes: Joshua saved Rahab the harlot alive... and she dwelleth in Israel even unto this day
the Canaanite harlot by faith made a daughter of the covenant
Rahab in the line of Christ (Matt. 1:5; James 2:25)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua adjured them when the city was burnt and spoiled, in a general way.
Keil & Delitzsch: the curse on the man who should build Jericho.

themes: Cursed be the man before the LORD, that riseth up and buildeth this city Jericho
the doomed city to lie a monument of judgment
the curse fulfilled in Hiel the Bethelite (1 Kings 16:34)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord counselled, directed, prospered, and succeeded him; the Targum, 'the Word of the Lord was for the help of Joshua' — the essential Word, Christ, the Captain of the host.

themes: So the LORD was with Joshua; and his fame was noised throughout all the country
the success of God's servant from his presence
I will be with thee (Josh. 1:5)
